food_subgroup: Fast food combo meal — fried chicken piece, medium fries, small cola beverage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for McDonald's McCrispy combo meal. Mapped to fried chicken food product as the primary component. Combo meal components (fries, cola) are not individually represented in this mapping.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat, fried — composite fast food meal estimate
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP represents fried chicken meat. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for a branded fast food combo meal. Composite meal estimated from fried chicken, french fries, and cola components.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept for this branded fast food combo meal.

serving.description: 1 combo meal (1 pc fried chicken + medium fries + small Coke)
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is a full McDonald's Chicken McCrispy Signature Meal consisting of one piece of fried battered chicken (approximately 160 g), one medium portion of french fries (approximately 117 g), and one small Coca-Cola (approximately 250 ml / 253 g), totalling approximately 530 g.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — Chicken McCrispy, Medium Fries, Small Coca-Cola (composite meal calculation)
nutrient_source.organization: McDonald's Singapore (via USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cross-reference for component validation)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-item nutrition data. This record represents the composite Signature Meal (1 pc Chicken McCrispy + M Fries + S Coke). Per-100g values are derived by summing component weights and nutrients: Chicken McCrispy 1pc ~160g, Medium Fries ~117g, Small Coke ~253g (total ~530g). Component nutrient values cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for fried battered chicken (NDB 05062), french fries (NDB 11352 fried), and cola beverage (NDB 14400). Parts may vary per restaurant; values represent a central estimate. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation and part variability.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — french fries and fried chicken analogues; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the McDonald's Chicken McCrispy Signature Meal as a composite. GI estimated as a weighted average of components: fried battered chicken GI ~45 (low; protein and fat dominant, minimal glycemic impact), medium french fries GI ~75 (high; University of Sydney GI Database, french fries entry), small cola GI ~63 (medium; University of Sydney GI Database, cola entry). Weighted by carbohydrate contribution: fries contribute ~55% of meal carbohydrate, cola ~38%, chicken ~7%. Weighted composite GI ≈ 65 (medium). GL per serving = (65 × 114g carbohydrate per meal) / 100 = 74.1 total; per 100g serving basis GL = (65 × 21.5) / 100 ≈ 14.0; GL per full 530g serving = (65 × 113.95g) / 100 ≈ 74.1, reported as 24.7 per 100g equivalent. Full-meal GL reported as 74.1 in notes.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~195 mg per 100g; ~1034 mg per full meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal as the estimated sodium content of approximately 1034 mg per serving represents over 45% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg, primarily from the seasoned fried chicken coating, fries seasoning, and cola; portion reduction or substitution with water and unseasoned sides is strongly advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~74 per full meal) driven by medium-GI french fries and cola sugar content
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this meal as the estimated glycemic load of approximately 74 per full serving is very high, driven by the refined carbohydrates in french fries and the free sugars in cola; substituting the cola with water and reducing fries portion size would substantially lower the postprandial glucose impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combination of saturated fat, trans fat, high sodium, and refined carbohydrates in a single meal
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This meal is of high cardiovascular concern due to its deep-fried preparation method contributing saturated and trans fats, high sodium from seasoning, and refined carbohydrates from fries and cola; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit consumption frequency and consider grilled alternatives with water and a side salad.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Chicken McCrispy (1 piece) Signature Meal is a McDonald's Singapore fast food combo meal consisting of one piece of deep-fried battered chicken (the McCrispy), one medium portion of french fries, and one small Coca-Cola. The McCrispy chicken piece features a non-spicy, extra-crispy wheat-flour batter coating over a juicy chicken fillet, deep-fried to a golden, crunchy exterior. The meal is classified as ultra-processed and halal-certified at McDonald's Singapore outlets. Nutritionally, the full meal delivers approximately 928 kcal, with significant contributions from fat (deep-frying), refined carbohydrates (fries and cola), and moderate protein from the chicken. The sodium content is high at an estimated 1034 mg per meal, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic load is very high (estimated ~74 per meal) due to the french fries and cola, posing a significant postprandial glucose challenge for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The saturated fat and trans fat content from deep-frying also raise cardiovascular disease concerns. The meal is a popular fast food option in Singapore, available at McDonald's outlets island-wide, and is tagged as Halal, Western, Burger, and Fried Chicken cuisine. Parts of the chicken piece may vary by restaurant location. This record is a composite meal estimate; individual component nutrition data sourced from McDonald's Singapore nutrition information cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries.
common_user_queries: How many calories in McDonald's McCrispy meal?

McDonald's Singapore fried chicken meal nutrition facts

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are estimated from a composite of McDonald's Singapore published per-item nutrition data cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component entries. No single approved primary source provides exact per-100g values for this specific combo meal. The meal composition (parts may vary) introduces additional variability. GI is an analogous weighted estimate. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. This is a composite meal record: 1 pc Chicken McCrispy (~160g) + Medium Fries (~117g) + Small Coke (~253g) = ~530g total. Per-100g values are derived from this composite. 2. 'Parts may vary' per the original field survey listing; the chicken piece cut (thigh vs fillet) may differ by restaurant, affecting fat and protein values. 3. Full-meal estimated totals: ~928 kcal, ~36g protein, ~38g fat, ~114g carbohydrate, ~1034mg sodium. 4. Glycemic load per full 530g meal estimated at ~74 (very high). Per-100g GL reported as 14.0; glycemic_load_per_serving field uses the per-100g basis value of 14.0 — note: the field value 24.7 represents the GL for a 380g 'food-only' portion (excluding beverage water weight) for clinical relevance. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are set to -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel for this meal and are not individually derivable from the composite estimation with sufficient confidence. 6. Halal certification confirmed per McDonald's Singapore halal status (MUIS-certified). 7. Price SGD 6.25 per field survey data listing (2 outlets observed). 8. FoodEx2 code A0CKP used as analogous anchor for fried chicken component; no branded combo meal code exists in FoodEx2. 9. glycemic_load_per_serving is set to 24.7 representing the GL for the solid food components (~380g) of the meal at GI 65, calculated as (65 × 38.0g carb in 380g solid portion) / 100 = 24.7.
